rulings.Case Title: GEORGE TYLER VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, January 15, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Moore Brothers' motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production because it fails to cite to any response to a contention interrogatory whether plaintiff was exposed to an asbestos-containing product attributable to the defendant. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM DALE BOND VS. A.W.CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, January 15, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Defendant Hill Brothers Chemical Company's motion for summary judgment is denied. Plaintiffs presented sufficient evidence to permit a reasonable fact-finder to draw the inference that some of the Flintkote tile that Mr. Bond worked with contained asbestos attributable to Hill Brothers. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: RANDOLPH WATSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, January 15, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 6. Defendant Velan Valve Corp.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Velan Valve's alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to the sophisticated intermediary defense (issues 1-3) and granted as to the claims for false representation and punitive damges (issues 4-5).. Velan Valve has not established all the elements of a sophisticated intermediary defense. There is no evidence that Velan Valve knew of Chevron's safety precautions such that it would have been reasonable for Velan Valve to rely on Chevron to protect its employees. (Willis v. Raymark Industries, Inc. (4th Cir. 1990) 905 F.2d 793.) Velan Valve satisfied its burden of production on the claims for false representation and punitive damages due to plaintiff's factually devoid special interrogatory responses. Plaintiff concedes that he has not offered any evidence creating a triable issue whether Velan Valve made an affirmative misrepresentation of material fact to plaintiff. Plaintiff has failed to provide admissible evidence to create a triable issue whether Velan Valve acted with fraud, malice, or oppression by clear and convincing evidence. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DONALD SALISBURY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, January 15, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 7. Defendant Metalclad Insulation Corporation's motion for summary judgment is denied. Metalclad 's alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to exposure (issues 1-4) and granted as to punitive damages (issue 5). Plaintiff presented sufficient evidence to permit a reasonable fact-finder to draw the inference that plaintiff was exposed to Pabco attributable to Metalclad. Metalclad's objections to the declarations of plaintiff and Charles Ay are overruled. Metalclad's objections to the prior testimony of Donald Trueblood, Wayne May and Russell Saunders are sustained. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL GARSKE VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, January 15, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 8. Defendant TOSCO REFINING COMPANY, INC.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. Plaintiff's responses to Tosco's contention interrogatories submitted with the opposition papers (which can be considered in determining whether Tosco satisfied its burden of production see Weil and Brown book at 10:251 and 10:271.1), plaintiff's deposition testimony and the contract between Tosco and Plant Maintenance satisfied Tosco's burden of production. Plaintiff's list of persons who might have knowledge without specific contact information does not preclude the shifting of the burden. Plaintiff failed to raise a triable issue regarding exposure to asbestos attributable to Tosco. The declarations of Hanisian and Ay are insufficient to show such exposure. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT HENRY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, January 15, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 9. Defendant THE GOODYEAR TIRE & RUBBER COMPANY's motion for summary judgment is continued to February 8, 2010. Plaintiff may file and e-serve any opposition to the substance of the motion no later than January 28, 2010. Defendant may file and e-serve any reply no later than February 3, 2010. While defendant timely e-served its moving papers, its decision to seek ex parte relief from its untimely personal service of those papers may have caused plaintiff to erroneously believe that the motion was untimely served. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: STEVEN CHARLES PRICE VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, January 15, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 10. Defendant Crane Co.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Plaintiff has presented sufficient evidence to create a triable issue regarding plaintiff's exposure to asbestos- containing products attributable to defendant. Defendant's D'Amico and other objections to plaintiff's declaration are overruled. Defendant's objections to the prior testimony of Mr. McLean are overruled. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: KENNETH UENO VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al

rulings.Case Title: KENNETH JOHNSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, January 15, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 12. Defendant NATIONAL STEEL AND SHIPBUILDING COMPANY's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to satisfy its burden of production on the issue of whether Johnson was directly exposed to asbestos-containing components on ships built by National Steel. Defendant's separate statement and citations to evidence therein focuses only on a re-entrainment theory of exposure and do not show that plaintiff lacks evidence to prove his direct exposure to an original component part of a ship manufactured by defendant. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: KENNETH JOHNSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, January 15, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 12, DEFENDANT ARMSTRONG INTERNATIONAL INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS OFF CALENDAR PER DISMISSAL FILED ON JANUARY 5, 2010. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: KENNETH JOHNSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, January 15, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 12. Defendant SB Decking Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant has not satisfied its burden of production on the evidence-free prong due to the absence of any responses to "all facts" interrogatories regarding plaintiff's exposure to asbestos-containing products attributable to defendant. Mr. Spaeth's declaration does not negate the ability of plaintiff to prove exposure. Even if defendant had satisfied its burden, plaintiff has created a triable issue regarding exposure by Marbley's declaration and defendant's interrogatory responses. Defendant's objections to Marbley's declaration are overruled. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: KENNETH GOFORTH VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, January 15, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 17. Defendant Cupertino Electric, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production by plaintiff's deposition and plaintiff's responses to contention interrogatories. The declarations of plaintiff and Ay do not create a triable issue that the fireproofing that plaintiff was exposed to contained asbestos =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: KENNETH GOFORTH VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, January 15, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 17. Defendant D. Zelinsky & Sons, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Plaintiff has presented sufficient evidence to raise a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing dust attributable to defendant. Defendant's objections to the declarations of plaintiff and Mr. Ay are overruled. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: KENNETH GOFORTH VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, January 15, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 17. Defendant Monterey Mechanical Company's motion for summary judgment is denied. Declarations of Robert Mingham and Charles Ay create a triable issue regarding exposure. There is no statutory authority to continue a summary motion at the moving party's request due to evidence submitted in opposition to the motion. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM M NEWELL et al VS. CLEAVER-BROOKS INC INDIVIDUALLY AND F/K/A et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, January 15, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 18. Defendant Honeywell International Inc., F/K/A ALLIEDSIGNAL INC.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production by plaintiff's responses to contention interrogatories. Plaintiff failed to provide any evidence admissible against Honeywell that creates a triable issue regarding Mr. Newell's exposure to any Honeywell/Bendix products. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM M NEWELL et al VS. CLEAVER-BROOKS INC INDIVIDUALLY AND F/K/A et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, January 15, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 18, DEFENDANT IMO INDUSTRIES, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS OFF CALENDAR PER REQUEST OF MOVING PARTY. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JERRY M PARKER VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, January 15, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 19. Defendant Ashby Lumber Company's motion for summary judgment is denied. Liberally construed, plaintiff's deposition testimony that he breathed visible dust from "asbestos tape" purchased from Ashby Lumber is sufficient to create a triable issue regarding exposure to an asbestos-containing product attributable to Ashby Lumber. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MELVIN RODRICK VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, January 15, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 20. Defendant W. R. Meadows Inc.'s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied. Defendant failed to satisfy its burden of production as to the evidence-free prong because it did not present any responses to contention interrogatories and the deposition of plaintiff alone is insufficient to satisfy the burden on that prong. Defendant also failed to present facts negating that it manufactured, supplied or distributed any asbestos-containing product because Dwyer's declaration provides insufficient basis for such an assertion. Even if defendant satisfied its burden of production, plaintiff created a triable issue of fact by plaintiff's declaration and the references to Seal Tight in the manhole infiltration disk and seal assembly patent information. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MELVIN RODRICK VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, January 15, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 20. Defendant Slakey Brothers, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Plaintiff created a triable issue of fact by the Leslie declaration. Defendant's objections to the Leslie declaration are overruled. =(220/HEK)
